history.titleZh: 谷歌25年来首次重新设计搜索框——其重要性远超你的想象
history.descriptionZh:
在长达四分之一个世纪的时间里，谷歌搜索框一直是计算领域最具辨识度的界面之一：一个细长的白色矩形，闪烁的光标，寥寥数语的打字输入，以及一列蓝色链接。然而，在周二的年度I/O开发者大会上，谷歌正式宣告这一范式的终结。公司宣布了对搜索框本身——这个每天发起数十亿次查询的文本字段——的彻底重新设计，将其从简单的关键词输入转变为由AI驱动的动态对话起点，能够接受文本、图像、PDF、视频甚至来自Chrome浏览器的标签页作为输入。同时，谷歌正在将其AI Overviews（AI概览）与AI Mode（AI模式）功能整合成一个无缝的搜索流程，消除了以前迫使用户在传统结果页和AI优先体验之间做出选择的摩擦。

谷歌搜索副总裁兼负责人Liz Reid在周一的新闻发布会上称其为“自25年前标志性搜索框诞生以来的最大升级”。这一消息伴随着大量其他新闻发布——包括新的Gemini模型、名为Spark的个人AI Agent、一个智能购物车以及一个重新构想的开发者平台——但搜索框的重新设计可能被证明是最具影响力的。它发出了迄今为止最明确的信号：谷歌将其旗舰产品的未来视为一个用户与一个由整个网络支持的人工智能系统进行开放式、多模态对话的界面，而非输入零散关键词的场所。

**新的搜索框：扩大、接受文件，并引导你提问**

这些变化显示出谷歌对人们如何与其创造公司Alphabet绝大多数收入的产品进行互动的根本性期望转变。搜索框现在可以动态扩展，以容纳更长、更对话式的查询。与过去那个微妙地鼓励简洁（狭小的字段适合两三个词的关键词串）的旧界面不同，新设计邀请用户以细致入微的细节充分阐述复杂的问题。它还直接支持多模态输入。用户可以直接从主搜索界面上传图像、PDF、文件和视频，或拖入来自Chrome标签页的内容。以前，其中一些功能存在于AI模式中，但需要额外的步骤才能访问。现在，它们位于主要的入口点。

谷歌还部署了一个它描述为“超越自动补全”的、由AI驱动的查询建议系统。该系统并非仅仅根据热门搜索来预测用户可能输入的下一个词，而是帮助用户构建复杂、微妙的查询——本质上是引导他们提出AI模式处理得最好的那种详细问题。新的搜索框将立即在AI模式可用的所有国家和语言中推出。

**整合AI Overviews和AI Mode：打造无缝体验**

或许比搜索框本身更重要的是其背后的架构变化。谷歌正在将AI Overviews（出现在传统搜索结果上方的AI生成摘要面板）与AI Mode（公司一年前在I/O上推出的更具沉浸感的对话式搜索体验）统一起来。从周二开始，这种融合的体验将在全球范围内的移动端和桌面端上线。用户可以输入一个问题，接收AI Overview以及传统结果，然后直接继续进入来回式的AI Mode对话以提出后续问题——所有这些都无需导航到单独的界面。

Reid在新闻发布会上解释了其中的逻辑：新的AI搜索框是“对我们传统搜索框的升级，因此结果会直接带你进入主搜索而非AI模式”。她指出，虽然一些高级用户会主动寻找AI模式，但“对于大多数用户来说，他们实际上并不想思考是想要一个更传统的页面还是一个AI优先的搜索体验”。目标是确保“对于大多数用户来说，他们不必考虑去哪里，只需要使用他们熟悉的搜索框，之后就能获得最佳体验”。

**十亿用户和翻倍的查询量：揭示搜索行为变化有多快**

谷歌决定重新设计其最重要产品的基础界面并非凭空而来。公司在发布会期间分享了一系列使用统计数据，揭示了用户行为的变化有多迅速。在2025年I/O大会上于美国推出的AI Mode，在推出第一年内月活用户已突破十亿。自推出以来，AI Mode的查询量每季度翻一番。更轻量级的AI摘要功能AI Overviews，每月用户已超过25亿。总体搜索查询量在上个季度达到了历史最高水平——这一数据点公司此前已在财报电话会议上披露。

谷歌CEO Sundar Pichai将这些数据视为AI功能对搜索使用量具有“增量”而非“蚕食”效应的证据。他表示，“当人们使用搜索中的AI驱动功能时，他们会更多地使用搜索”。他补充说，他喜欢“搜索如何变得不再像单个查询，而更像是一场持续的对话，给用户带来更深入的见解，并将你与浩瀚的网络连接起来”。Reid进一步强调了这一点：“不仅仅是人们在搜索得更多，而是他们搜索的方式变了。他们正在用细致入微的细节充分表达问题，提出后续问题，并在不同模态之间进行搜索。”

**Gemini 3.5 Flash：为AI搜索提供规模化所需的速度**

在幕后，新的搜索体验运行在谷歌最新的人工智能模型Gemini 3.5 Flash上，该模型同样在I/O大会上发布。谷歌将AI Mode的底层模型升级为3.5 Flash，以提供Reid所说的“更强大的AI搜索体验”。Gemini 3.5 Flash是今年一系列公告中的主力模型。谷歌声称，它在几乎所有基准测试上都优于其先前的前沿模型Gemini 3.1 Pro，同时每秒输出token的速度比同类前沿模型快四倍。Pichai将其描述为在“Artificial Analysis”指数（该指数将智能与速度进行对比绘制）的“右上象限中独树一帜”，意味着它以显著更低的延迟提供了接近前沿的质量。

这种速度对于搜索来说至关重要。对于一个每天处理数十亿次查询的产品来说，感觉迟缓的对话式AI搜索体验将毫无生命力。通过将重新设计的界面与既优化质量又优化吞吐量的模型相结合，谷歌试图让AI驱动的搜索感觉像旧的关键词体验一样即时——同时功能却强大得多。

**搜索现在可以即时生成交互式视觉元素和自定义迷你应用**

重新设计的搜索框也是一系列新功能的入口，将搜索远远推向了基于文本的答案之外。谷歌宣布了所谓的“生成式UI”——搜索能够实时动态地构建自定义小部件、交互式可视化，甚至是迷你应用程序，并针对用户的具体问题量身定制。Reid在发布会上提供了一个具体例子：用户可以问“黑洞如何影响时空？”，然后在AI Overview中收到一个交互式可视化图表，将这一概念生动呈现。后续问题会触发系统实时动态生成全新的视觉元素。她解释说，这是因为“我们与Google DeepMind团队合作构建了一个基于Gemini 3.5 Flash运行的新型实时代码生成系统”。生成式UI功能将于今年夏天向所有人免费推出。

但谷歌走得更远。对于持续进行的任务——比如策划婚礼、组织搬家、追踪健身计划——用户将能够在搜索中构建公司所说的可定制的、有状态（stateful）的体验，这些体验由其Antigravity开发平台提供支持。这不需要任何编码专业知识。用户只需用自然语言描述他们想要什么，搜索就会构建出来。这些体验将在未来几个月内推出，首先面向美国的Google AI Pro和Ultra订阅用户。

**全天候监控网络的AI Agent将进入搜索结果**

重新设计还为谷歌所称的“信息代理”打开了大门——用户可以直接在搜索中配置这些AI Agent，使其全天候监控网络上的特定条件，并在满足条件时提供综合更新。例如，用户可以设置一个Agent，用特定参数跟踪某个特定领域的市场波动。该Agent会创建一个监控计划，接入实时金融数据，并在条件满足时主动通知用户——同时提供用于进一步研究的链接和背景信息。其他用例包括寻找公寓、跟踪限量版球鞋发售，或监控用户关心的任何话题。信息代理将于今年夏天首先面向Google AI Pro和Ultra订阅用户推出。

这些Agent置于谷歌在发布会期间阐述的一个更大的战略转变之中：该公司正全力投入AI系统，这些系统不仅能回答问题，还能主动代表用户采取行动。除了搜索之外，谷歌还推出了Gemini Spark，一个全天候运行在Google Cloud专用虚拟机上的个人AI Agent；揭晓了通用购物车（Universal Cart），一个智能的跨商家购物车；宣布了用于Agent进行安全购买的Agent支付协议；并将其Antigrowth开发者平台扩展为一个构建自主AI Agent的完整生态系统。

**出版商、广告商和SEO专业人士面临新现实**

重新设计对围绕旧有模式（关键词搜索和蓝色链接）构建的庞大生态系统——出版商、广告商、SEO专业人士——提出了深刻的问题。如果用户越来越多地以完整的对话句子而非零散的关键词来表达他们的需求，那么整个搜索引擎优化的学科都需要进化。当AI解析的是自然语言意图而非匹配字符串时，关键词密度策略就变得不那么重要了。以权威方式回答深刻、微妙问题的内容变得更有价值；而为了在短关键词片段上排名而设计的内容则价值降低。

对于出版商来说，风险攸关。AI Overviews已经从整个网络综合信息并直接呈现在搜索结果中，减少了用户点击来源材料的需求。新的无缝AI模式整合加深了这一动态：用户现在可以在不离开搜索页面的情况下获得AI生成的答案并提出多个后续问题。谷歌一直坚持其AI功能为出版商带来了更多流量，但随着搜索结果页面变得更加自给自足，这一说法受到了新的审视。

对于资助谷歌绝大多数收入的广告商而言，从关键词到对话的转变改变了广告定位的考量。对话式查询包含更丰富的意图信号，这可以使广告定位更精确、更有价值。但它们也带来了新的模糊性：当用户处于与AI Mode的多轮对话中间时，广告自然适合放在哪里？谷歌在发布会期间没有详细说明其广告模式的变化，但界面的结构性转变将不可避免地重塑广告的展示和衡量方式。

**搜索框从来都不仅仅是一个产品：它是数十亿人的习惯**

谷歌选择重新设计搜索框，而不仅仅是在其背后添加新功能，是有原因的。搜索框目前不仅仅是一个产品元素；它是一个文化产物——是所有接入互联网的世界使用的少数几项数字基础设施之一。改变它发出了一个关于公司认为计算未来走向的明确信息。25年来，搜索框训练了数十亿人用关键词思考——将他们的好奇心压缩成尽可能短的词语串。新的搜索框邀请他们做相反的事情：大声思考，上传他们正在看的东西，提出后续问题，让一个AI系统来处理压缩工作。

Pichai将公司的更广泛雄心与一个惊人的统计数据联系起来：谷歌的各类产品界面现在每月处理超过3.2万亿个token，比一年前增加了七倍。该公司预计2026年的资本支出约为1800亿至1900亿美元，大约是四年前310亿美元的六倍，这主要是为了支持这一AI转型所需的基础设施。当被问及传统搜索的未来时，他直言不讳。“搜索是世界上使用最多的AI产品，”他说。谷歌搜索框中闪烁的光标仍然邀请你打字。但在教导世界用关键词说话了25年之后，谷歌现在要求它用句子说话——并押注大约1900亿美元，相信它会的。

history.title: Would you let robots spend your money? Google is betting on it
history.description:
Google is going all in on AI-driven shopping even as some competitors back off. 
At Google I/O, the company unveiled the latest iteration of its AI commerce tools: a "Universal Cart" that works across different retailers and Google products like Gemini - and eventually YouTube and Gmail, too. Users can add products to Google's universal cart as they browse Search and chat with Gemini and then check out through Google. The cart will also track prices, provide in-stock notifications, suggest potential discounts, and alert shoppers to potential issues with their selections. 
Despite the transformative changes AI has brought to the workplace,  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: Google is going all in on AI-driven shopping even as some competitors back off.At Google I/O, the company unveiled the latest iteration of its AI commerce tools: a “Universal Cart” that works across different retailers and Google products like Gemini — and eventually YouTube and Gmail, too. Users can add products to Google’s universal cart as they browse Search and chat with Gemini and then check out through Google. The cart will also track prices, provide in-stock notifications, suggest potential discounts, and alert shoppers to potential issues with their selections.Despite the transformative changes AI has brought to the workplace, business, and culture, tech companies are still trying to make the case to the average person that AI can improve their lives or make tedious, unpleasant tasks easier. One place Google thinks that could be is shopping. In November, the company introduced a way for shoppers to dispatch an AI voice to call brick-and-mortar stores to ask about inventory; it also began rolling out a semi-automatic way for shoppers to have AI agents purchase items online on their behalf.The Universal Cart attempts to corral people’s shopping habits into one place. People shop over the course of days, across different devices and accounts, says Vidhya Srinivasan, vice president and general manager of ads and commerce at Google.“A lot of the ways I capture this is by having many, many, many tabs open and by syncing profiles and things like that. And it kind of works,” Srinivasan said in an exclusive briefing. “What the shopping cart does from a current problem perspective is it brings all of this together … It is a cart that’s going to be available wherever I am across Google properties.” A cart icon will be displayed next to a user’s profile picture.The Universal Cart works across retailers and across Google surfaces.Srinivasan envisions the cart almost like a personal shopper working in the background. The Universal Cart works across different retailers, including Sephora, Target, Wayfair, and Walmart, and eventually users will be able to add items to their cart from YouTube or when they see products in Gmail. Once a product is in the cart, users can get price-drop alerts, view price history, and be notified when an out-of-stock item is available again. Srinivasan says the cart — which runs on Gemini — can also alert a user to potential issues with their planned purchases. She gives the example of someone building their first PC choosing a motherboard and processor with incompatible sockets without realizing it; the cart would flag the discrepancy and warn the shopper of potential problems. Shoppers can also connect retailer loyalty programs and credit cards through Google Pay, and the Universal Cart will suggest payment methods and potential ways to save money. If a shopper wants to build a cart but doesn’t want to check out through Google, they can also transfer the contents of their cart to a retailer’s website and finish checkout there.“The retailer might have other things they want to show the person when they land over there, and they can go deeper in other ways potentially,” Srinivasan says.Agentic shopping is only possible — and helpful — with the buy-in from a variety of actors: search engines, retailers, payment processors, and so on. Participation from retailers is especially important, considering widespread adoption of agentic shopping could mean customers have little reason to actually visit a store’s website at all (we’ve been calling this “the Doordash problem” at The Verge). Amazon sued AI company Perplexity in November for allowing users to buy products through its Comet AI browser. OpenAI’s efforts at checkout features within ChatGPT were disappointing. As more shoppers use AI chatbots to research products to buy or get recommendations, getting surfaced in AI search platforms is becoming more and more urgent for retailers and brands, which are already tweaking their online presence to try to get chatbots to mention them.Google seems to know it is getting between merchants and their customers; Srinivasan says the company is “very focused” on the value exchange between all parties. “[Consumers] benefit, but also merchants benefit, because [in the] long run, that’s the only way it works,” she says.Srinivasan describes Google’s place in the interaction as a “matchmaker”Having billions of products available for purchase within Gemini is great for Google, but retailers need something in return. Srinivasan says Google does not currently take a cut of sales or a commission for products purchased. I asked Srinivasan whether she’s heard concerns from retailers about the idea that Google could become the portal through which shoppers buy things online. She describes Google’s place in the interaction as a “matchmaker.”“We really want to facilitate lots of consumers talking to lots of merchants,” Srinivasan says. “We don’t want to be the merchant of record.”On the infrastructure level, there are signs that the retail industry is coalescing around Google. In January, the company announced the Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP), a new open standard it developed with major retailers like Walmart, Shopify, and Target that makes the entire AI shopping journey possible: researching items, putting them in a cart, buying them, paying for them, and getting post-purchase customer service. (OpenAI has its own competing version.) In April, Amazon, Meta, Microsoft, Salesforce, and Stripe joined the committee that governs UCP.The Universal Cart also gives users price insights.Google previously introduced a way for shoppers to purchase products directly within AI Mode in Search and in the Gemini app, and it’s now expanding into hotel and local food delivery categories. Using Gemini Spark, a new “24/7 personal AI agent” announced by Google, users will also be able to give AI agents more specific guidelines for purchases, like the brands they like, items they’re looking for, and budget. The shopping agent can then make purchases on the shopper’s behalf, provided all criteria are met. A shopper could specify the exact model of a pair of boots they want, for example, set a price limit, and have the AI agent purchase the item when it finds it. The purchases use a technology called Agent Payments Protocol (AP2), essentially a digital paper trail and approval process for having an AI agent carry out a task like buying something.Shopping is complicated — what happens, for example, when the robot buys something under the price a user specifies, but with tax and shipping it ends up being much more expensive than another option? Would most shoppers trust AI enough to spend their money on their behalf? (Srinivasan tells me Google is currently working through all of this, but that in general, a shopper would go to the actual retailer, not Google, to resolve problems after purchase. Which raises another question: Will retailers introduce policies around purchases made with AI?) Buying things is also emotional: If a rare item on my wishlist pops up for $4 more than I told the chatbot was my limit, I might pull the trigger even if the robot couldn’t. It is hard to imagine a world where shoppers immediately outsource their shopping to a machine: It would be a radical reshaping of what it means to buy things. Most of all, adoption would require an enormous amount of consumer trust — and that is still an uphill battle.
history.author: Mia Sato
history.titleZh: 你会让机器人帮你花钱吗？谷歌正在押注这一点
history.descriptionZh:
谷歌在刚刚举行的Google I/O大会上发布了最新一代AI购物工具——“通用购物车”（Universal Cart），这标志着该公司在AI驱动购物领域全面加码，即便部分竞争对手正在收缩战线。该购物车不仅能在不同零售商之间无缝切换，还能与Gemini等谷歌产品联动，未来甚至将整合YouTube和Gmail。用户在使用谷歌搜索或与Gemini聊天时，可将商品加入这个统一的购物车，并通过谷歌完成结账。此外，购物车还具备价格追踪、库存到货提醒、折扣建议以及购买兼容性问题预警等功能。

AI虽已深刻改变工作、商业和文化，但科技公司仍面临向普通人证明AI能改善生活、减轻琐碎任务负担的挑战。谷歌认为，购物正是AI大显身手的领域之一。去年11月，谷歌曾推出AI语音助手，让用户能通过语音拨打实体店电话询问库存；同时，还开始逐步部署半自动AI代理，帮助用户在线代购商品。

“通用购物车”的核心目标是整合用户分散的购物行为。谷歌广告与商务副总裁兼总经理Vidhya Srinivasan指出，人们往往在数天、跨不同设备和账户完成购物，而当前的做法是打开无数标签页、同步多个账户资料，这“勉强可行”。而通用购物车则将所有环节汇集到一处，无论在谷歌哪个产品中，都会有一个购物车图标显示在用户头像旁。该购物车可在多个零售商（如丝芙兰、塔吉特、Wayfair和沃尔玛）以及谷歌各个平台间通用。未来，用户从YouTube或Gmail中看到商品时也能直接加入购物车。

Srinivasan将购物车描述成一个在后台运作的“私人购物助理”。它基于Gemini运行，能提醒用户价格下跌，展示价格历史，当缺货商品重新上架时发出通知。更重要的是，它还能预警购买计划中的潜在问题。例如，用户初次组装电脑时，可能误选不兼容的主板和CPU插座，购物车会提示差异并警告潜在问题。用户可以连接零售商忠诚度计划和信用卡至Google Pay，购物车会推荐最省钱的支付方式。如果用户不想通过谷歌结账，也能将购物车内容转移到零售商网站完成交易。Srinivasan表示，这样零售商可以在用户登陆后展示更多内容，深入互动。

“代理式购物”（agentic shopping）的实现有赖于搜索引擎、零售商、支付处理商等多方参与。零售商的支持尤为关键，因为大规模采用代理购物可能使用户几乎不再访问零售商自己的网站——Verge将此称为“DoorDash问题”。亚马逊去年11月曾起诉AI公司Perplexity，因其允许用户通过其Comet AI浏览器直接购买商品。OpenAI在ChatGPT内嵌入结账功能的尝试也令人失望。随着越来越多用户通过AI聊天机器人研究商品或获取推荐，零售商和品牌正加紧调整线上呈现，以便被AI搜索平台提及。

谷歌显然意识到自己正介入商家与消费者之间。Srinivasan称公司非常关注各方之间的价值交换，消费者和商家都能从中受益，这是可持续的唯一方式。她将谷歌的角色描述为“媒人”——谷歌不会从中抽取销售提成或佣金。当被问及零售商是否担心谷歌成为在线购物的“门户”时，Srinivasan强调：“我们真正想做的是促进大量消费者与众多商家对话，而不是成为记录在案的商家。”

基础设施层面已有迹象表明零售行业正围绕谷歌整合。今年1月，谷歌联合沃尔玛、Shopify、塔吉特等主要零售商推出了通用商务协议（Universal Commerce Protocol, UCP），这是一个新的开放标准，支持完整的AI购物流程：研究商品、加入购物车、购买、支付、以及售后客服。（OpenAI也有自己的竞争版本。）4月，亚马逊、Meta、微软、Salesforce和Stripe加入了UCP的管理委员会。

通用购物车还提供价格洞察。谷歌此前已允许用户在搜索AI模式和Gemini应用中直接购买商品，现正向酒店和本地外卖等品类扩展。通过谷歌新推出的“24/7个人AI代理”Gemini Spark，用户可以给AI代理设定更具体的采购指南，如偏好的品牌、寻找的商品和预算。一旦满足所有条件，购物代理可代表用户完成购买。例如，用户可以指定某款靴子的具体型号，设置价格上限，当AI代理找到满足条件的商品时自动下单。这类购买采用名为“代理支付协议”（Agent Payments Protocol, AP2）的技术，本质上是一套数字记录和审批流程，用于让AI代理执行购买等任务。

然而，购物本身极其复杂。比如，AI按用户设定价格买下一件商品，但加上税金和运费后总价远超其他选项怎么办？大多数用户会信任AI来替自己花钱吗？Srinivasan表示，谷歌正在研究这些细节，但通常用户购买后的问题会直接找零售商而不是谷歌解决。这又引出另一个问题：零售商是否会针对AI完成的购买制定特殊政策？此外，购物带有情感因素：如果愿望清单上的一件稀有商品价格比用户设定的上限高出4美元，用户可能会“破例”下单，但AI不会。很难想象用户会迅速将购物行为外包给机器——那将彻底改变“购买”的含义。最重要的是，普及需要巨大的消费者信任，而这仍然是一场艰难的战斗。

history.title: Gmail is going to start talking to you
history.description:
Google is launching a big new feature for Gmail called Gmail Live, a new AI-powered voice mode that's basically the Gemini Live experience but built specifically for your inbox.
To use Gmail Live, tap an icon that will appear in your search bar and just start talking. In a press briefing, a Google employee showed a live demo of the feature where she asked questions about things like events at her kid's school and an upcoming trip to Detroit. Gmail pulled up relevant details in the Gmail Live interface, like the date and location of a show-and-tell event at the school, all sourced from the employee's inbox.
It was an intriguing demo, and yo …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: Google is launching a big new feature for Gmail called Gmail Live, a new AI-powered voice mode that’s basically the Gemini Live experience but built specifically for your inbox.To use Gmail Live, tap an icon that will appear in your search bar and just start talking. In a press briefing, a Google employee showed a live demo of the feature where she asked questions about things like events at her kid’s school and an upcoming trip to Detroit. Gmail pulled up relevant details in the Gmail Live interface, like the date and location of a show-and-tell event at the school, all sourced from the employee’s inbox.Here’s an example of what Gmail Live might show you. Image: GoogleIt was an intriguing demo, and you can see how Gmail Live could be useful; when searching, instead of wading through the usual intimidating list of emails while you’re searching for one specific detail, you could just ask Gmail Live to figure it out and tell you what you’re looking for. But that means the feature must be trustworthy — if it’s not accurate in a pinch, like pulling up a flight’s confirmation code in a hurry at the airport, then people probably won’t use it.“Trust is the bedrock of how we think about Gmail in general,” Blake Barnes, VP of product for Gmail, tells The Verge. He says the company has spent a lot of time figuring out how, in every step of using Gmail Live, “you’re getting a product you can trust,” even when it’s trying to help you with complex queries. “It’s our job to take that complexity away from you in a way that you can trust and rely on us.” Gmail Live will also be able to show you the source where it’s getting its information, which lets you verify what it’s telling you.Google is bringing voice-driven AI features to Docs and Keep, too. With Docs Live, you can talk over your ideas with Gemini and it will help structure a document and pull in details from places like your Gmail and Google Drive. In Keep, as you talk about things you might want the app to track, it can put together things like reminders and grocery lists for you. Gmail Live, Docs Live, and the voice-driven Keep features will roll out on mobile starting this summer to subscribers of Google’s AI Pro and Ultra tiers (though the Keep upgrade will roll out first just on Android).Google is also expanding availability of Gmail’s AI Inbox, which basically turns your inbox into an email version of Google Search’s AI Mode, to subscribers of its Pro and Plus tiers. It’s already available for Ultra subscribers.
history.author: Jay Peters
history.descriptionZh:
Google宣布为Gmail推出一项重大新功能——Gmail Live，这是一种基于AI的语音交互模式，本质上是将Gemini Live体验深度集成到邮箱场景中。用户只需点击搜索栏中的图标即可开始语音提问。在媒体发布会上，Google员工进行了现场演示：她询问关于孩子学校活动和即将前往底特律的行程信息，Gmail Live界面立即从收件箱中提取出相关细节，例如学校展示活动的日期和地点。该功能的核心创新在于将AI语音助手与邮件数据高效结合，摒弃传统邮件搜索中需要浏览大量邮件的流程，直接通过自然语言对话定位特定信息。

技术原理上，Gmail Live利用Gemini模型的语义理解能力，实时解析用户语音指令，并从个人收件箱中精准检索并结构化呈现关键数据。其关键创新点在于“可信赖性”——Google产品副总裁Blake Barnes强调，信任是Gmail设计的基石，团队在每一步交互中都确保用户能获得可靠的产品，即使面对复杂查询也能依赖其输出。为增强可信度，Gmail Live会显示信息来源，允许用户验证结果准确性。

性能方面，现场演示展示了极快的响应速度和准确提取能力（如学校活动的时间地点）。虽然新闻未提供具体对比数据，但隐含着与手动搜索相比效率的显著提升，尤其适用于紧急场景（如机场快速查找航班确认码）。不过，准确性是用户采用的关键门槛，如果误判则可能失去信任。

技术影响与应用场景：Gmail Live主要解决邮件信息快速提取痛点，适用于旅行规划、追踪日程、查找凭证等场景。同时，Google将语音AI扩展到Docs和Keep：Docs Live允许用户与Gemini口述想法，自动生成文档结构并整合来自Gmail和Google Drive的信息；Keep则可通过语音创建提醒和购物清单。这些功能均于今夏向Google AI Pro和Ultra订阅用户开放移动端，Keep功能初期仅限Android。此外，Gmail的AI收件箱功能（类似Google搜索AI模式的邮箱版）已向Ultra用户开放，并扩展至Pro和Plus订阅用户。这些举措标志着Google正将AI语音交互深度嵌入生产力工具生态，旨在通过自然语言降低信息处理复杂度，同时强调透明度和用户可控性。

history.title: Intel tells PC makers to adopt 18A CPUs or lose their supply, report claims — Intel 7 supply dries up, pressuring notebook and PC manufacturers in the US, China, and Taiwan
history.description: Intel has redirected its constrained Intel 7 capacity toward server and industrial customers.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 14 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Intel is pressuring notebook and PC manufacturers in the U.S., China, and Taiwan to build more systems around its 18A-based processors, according to a Nikkei Asia report published today. The company has effectively frozen additional supply of older Intel 7-based CPUs for the consumer market, multiple industry sources told the publication, leaving OEMs with the choice of designing around 18A or going without.Go deeper with TH Premium: CPU (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU scaling with DLSSRyzen to the top: How AMD innovated in the gaming CPU marketHow ARM is working its way into PCsAMD CES 2026 gaming trends press Q&A roundtable transcriptThe push covers Intel's Panther Lake (Core Ultra Series 3) and Wildcat Lake (Core Series 3) families, both manufactured on 18A. Intel told partners that the supply of those chips is healthier than for its older Alder Lake, Raptor Lake, and Arrow Lake products.Intel has also reportedly redirected its constrained Intel 7 capacity toward server and industrial customers, where margins are significantly higher. One consumer PC executive said that industrial-use CPU margins run roughly 20% above consumer equivalents, and that obtaining further Intel 7 allocations has become effectively impossible.Latest Videos From You may like PC makers face shortages of Intel and AMD CPUs that stretch up to six months ‘CPUs are cool again,' Intel and AMD reporting spikes in CPU demand due to agentic AI CPU requirements for AI workloads are multiplying, driving intensifying shortages and price hikes Intel 7 still underpins a large portion of Intel’s product line, from consumer notebook and desktop CPUs like Raptor Lake to Xeon 6 “Granite Rapids” server processors. As AI-driven demand for data center CPUs surged through 2025, Intel deliberately shifted Intel 7 wafer starts toward its Data Center and AI group, where Xeon parts can bring higher ASPs and better margins. CFO David Zinsner confirmed as much during Intel’s Q3 earnings call last October, when he said capacity constraints on Intel 7 and 10 had limited the company’s ability to meet demand across both data center and client products. Intel currently has no plans to expand Intel 7 capacity.Another exec described placing an order for 100 Intel 7 processors and receiving just 30, with 10 of those being unrequested 18A-based chips. “We were told if we don't take the 18A CPUs, they would be given to other PC makers.” Intel, in a statement to Nikkei Asia, described its Core Series 3 processors as “integral” to its client strategy but didn’t confirm whether it is actively steering clients toward 18A adoption.Many PC makers had originally built only a handful of 18A-based models to support Intel's launch rather than in response to consumer demand, the report claims. "Frankly speaking, PC makers designed a few models based on 18A last year mainly as a favor to Intel, as the chip is expensive and the market demand is relatively small because it is too premium," another source reportedly said.According to this report, that’s now changed, however, as OEMs that want CPU allocation are effectively being forced to redesign more of their lineups around the newer, pricier silicon. These designs, per one executive, will take “at least three months” to complete and verify, and the shift to premium CPUs also forces upgrades to displays, sensors, and other components to justify the price tag. Wildcat Lake launched barely a month ago, meaning Intel is asking OEMs to commit volume to a product family with almost no commercial track record. Beyond managing the shortage, Zinsner said during the same earnings call that 18A yields are adequate for supply but not yet sufficient to deliver healthy margins, with industry yields not expected until 2027. Pushing more OEM volume through 18A gives Intel the high-volume production data it needs to bring those costs down faster.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsS.Y. Hsu, co-CEO of AsusTek, confirmed during a recent earnings call that the company is prioritizing shipments of higher-end models in response to CPU and memory chip supply pressure.Counterpoint Research analyst Brady Wang said that demand continues to outpace supply, and that some of that pressure may be offset by weakening PC demand overall. Some in the industry expect a year-on-year decline of more than 15% as component costs rise. history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: 英特尔告知PC制造商采用18A CPU否则将失去供应，报告称——Intel 7供应枯竭，给美国、中国和台湾的笔记本及PC制造商带来压力
history.descriptionZh:
英特尔正通过限制其成熟Intel 7工艺处理器的供应，向美国、中国和台湾地区的笔记本及PC制造商施压，要求它们围绕其最新的18A制程处理器（包括Panther Lake和Wildcat Lake系列）进行系统设计。据《日经亚洲》报道，多位业内人士透露，英特尔实际上已冻结了对消费市场Intel 7 CPU的额外供应，这使得OEM厂商面临选择：要么设计基于18A的产品，要么面临无货可用的局面。英特尔已将受限的Intel 7产能转向利润更高的服务器和工业客户，一位消费PC高管表示，工业级CPU的利润率比消费级高出约20%，目前获取额外的Intel 7分配几乎已不可能。

Intel 7工艺仍是英特尔产品线的基石，覆盖从Raptor Lake等消费级CPU到Xeon 6“Granite Rapids”服务器处理器。随着2025年AI驱动的数据中心CPU需求激增，英特尔刻意将Intel 7晶圆启动量转向数据中心与AI部门，因为Xeon系列能带来更高的平均售价（ASP）和更好的利润率。首席财务官David Zinsner在去年10月的电话会议上证实，Intel 7和10工艺的产能限制制约了公司满足数据中心和客户端产品需求的能力，且英特尔目前无计划扩大Intel 7产能。

一位高管描述了这样的场景：订购100颗Intel 7处理器，实际只收到30颗，其中10颗竟是未要求的18A芯片。“我们被告知，如果不接受18A CPU，它们就会分给其他PC厂商。”英特尔在声明中称其Core Series 3处理器（基于18A）是客户端战略的“核心”，但未确认是否主动引导客户转向18A。

报道指出，许多PC厂商最初只为配合英特尔发布而设计了少量18A机型，并非基于消费者需求。“坦白说，PC厂商去年设计几款18A机型主要是帮英特尔一个忙，因为该芯片价格昂贵，且市场很小——过于高端了。”但现在情况已变：OEM若想获得CPU配额，实际上被迫围绕更新、更贵的芯片重新设计更多产品线。这类设计从完成到验证至少需要三个月，而转向高端CPU也迫使厂商升级显示屏、传感器等组件以合理化定价。值得注意的是，Wildcat Lake刚刚发布一个多月，英特尔等于要求OEM承诺量产一款几乎无商业记录的产品。

除了管理短缺，Zinsner在同一电话会议上指出，18A的良率虽足以供应，但尚未能带来健康的利润率，行业预计要到2027年才能实现。向18A推动更多OEM产能，能让英特尔获得所需的高产量数据以加速成本下降。华硕联席CEO徐世昌在最近的财报电话会议上证实，由于CPU和内存芯片供应压力，公司正优先出货高端型号。Counterpoint Research分析师Brady Wang表示，需求持续超过供应，但部分压力可能被总体PC需求疲软所抵消，随着组件成本上升，业内预计PC出货量同比下滑超过15%。

history.title: MSI Raider 16 Max HX review: Elite gaming performance
history.description: Gamers seeking maximum performance will find MSI’s Raider 16 Max HX a natural choice, particularly with the RTX 5090, as long as they're willing to pay up for it.
history.content: MSI’s Raider 16 Max HX (starting at $2,999) is one of the fastest gaming laptops, featuring an Intel Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus CPU and up to an Nvidia RTX 5090. Besides performance, this laptop delivers a vibrant OLED display and lasts much longer on battery than expected.While it’s missing a mechanical keyboard and could use a faster storage drive, it remains a viable pick if you’ve got the cash – our RTX 5090 review model is priced at a lofty $4,099.Design of the MSI Raider 16 Max HXMSI Raider 16 Max HX at Micro Center for $3,499.99The Raider’s thick chassis, flashy LED lighting, and lid graphics make no attempt to hide its gaming pedigree. The light bar along the front edge of the chassis and the shield logo on the lid back are customizable for effects and colors in the SteelSeries GG app. The laptop pulls off an aggressive look without overdoing it. Ignoring its size, there’s not a lot to attract the eye with the lighting disabled.Latest Videos FromImage 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Measuring 14.29 by 10.62 by 1.14 inches (WDH), the Raider occupies a similar footprint compared to the Alienware 16 Area-51 (14.37 by 11.41 by 1.12 inches) and Asus ROG Strix Scar 16 (13.94 x 10.55 x 0.90 inches). However, the MSI is easily the lightest of the group, at 5.73 pounds versus Alienware’s 7.49 and Asus’ 6.28 pounds.The Raider's construction might have something to do with that – while the chassis feels solid enough, it definitely feels plasticky. The lid is aluminum, but it feels thin and flexes more than I expected. At least the lid’s hinges are smooth and sturdy.MSI’s port selection is practical, with ports on three sides. On the left are two Thunderbolt 4 (USB-C) ports and an SD card reader; on the right, two USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 and an audio jack; and on the back, 2.5 Gbps Ethernet, HDMI 2.1, and another USB-A port. The 400 W power brick attaches to a USB-like proprietary connector on the back. The laptop also features a cable lock slot on the left edge. Inside, the Raider utilizes a Killer BE1750 networking card supporting the latest Wi-Fi 7 and Bluetooth 5.4 standards.Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)MSI Raider 16 Max HX SpecificationsSwipe to scroll horizontallyCPUIntel Core Ultra 9 290HX PlusGraphicsNvidia GeForce RTX 5090 (24GB GDDR7, 1,597 MHz boost clock, 175 W maximum graphics power)Memory32GB DDR5-6400 (2x 16GB)Storage1TB PCIe 4.0 SSD (Micron 2500)Display16-inch, 2560 x 1600, OLED, 240 HzNetworkingIntel Killer Wi-Fi BE1750 Wi-Fi 7, Bluetooth 5.4Ports2x Thunderbolt 4, 3x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2, HDMI 2.1, SD card reader, 2.5 Gbps EthernetCameraFHD IRBattery92 WhrPower Adapter400 W (proprietary connector)Operating SystemWindows 11 HomeDimensions (WxDxH)14.29 x 10.62 x 1.14 inches (363 x 270 x 29 mm)Weight5.73 pounds (2.6 kg)Price (as configured)$4,099Today's best MSI Raider 16 Max HX deals $3,499.99View No price informationCheck Best Buy No price informationCheck AmazonWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricesGaming and Graphics on the MSI Raider 16 Max HXWe tested the Raider 16 Max HX in flagship form, featuring a Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus processor, RTX 5090 graphics card, and 32GB of RAM.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsPlaying F1 24 at 2560 x 1600 with Ultra High settings and DLSS disabled, I saw the game average in the mid to low 90s when monitoring frames per second. The Raider didn’t skip a beat.The high-end gaming laptops in our comparison lineup include the Alienware 16 Area-51 (Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus, RTX 5080 175 W, $4,309), Asus’ ROG Strix Scar 16 (Core Ultra 9 275HX, RTX 5080 175 W, $3,299), and Razer’s last-gen Blade 18 (Core Ultra 9 275HX, RTX 5090 175 W, $5,199). All share the same class of CPU, with our MSI and the Alienware sporting the Arrow Lake Refresh chips, and all use an Nvidia RTX graphics card with a maximum graphics power rating of 175 W.Our comparisons focus on FPS at 1920 x 1080, but we also included results at each laptop’s native resolution, which was 2560 x 1600 for all except the Razer’s 3840 x 2400.Image 1 of 6(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)On Shadow of the Tomb Raider at the Highest detail preset, the Raider’s 197 FPS at 1080p was second only to the Razer (213 FPS) and edged ahead of the Alienware (191 FPS). The Raider produced the best numbers at native resolution, with 134 FPS next to the Alienware’s 116 FPS and the Asus’ 112 FPS. These differences are slight but show that the RTX 5090, which is also present in the Razer, offers a touch more performance than the RTX 5080 found in the Alienware and Asus.Running Cyberpunk 2077 at the Ray Tracing Ultra preset, the RTX 5090 laptops continued distinguishing themselves, with the Raider again topping the charts. Its 70 FPS was just behind the Razer (74 FPS) but well ahead of the Alienware (63 FPS) and Asus (61 FPS). It maintained that lead at native resolution, producing 42 FPS against Alienware’s 39 FPS and Asus’ 35 FPS.Moving to Far Cry 6 at the game’s Ultra preset, these laptops produced mostly indistinguishable performance, though the Raider slightly edged ahead of the Alienware and Asus at 1080p.The Raider continued its strong performance in Red Dead Redemption 2 at Medium settings, where its 130 FPS at 1080p outpaced the Alienware’s 123 FPS but was just shy of Razer’s 132 FPS. Meanwhile, it dominated native resolution, with 90 FPS against the 77 FPS each produced by Alienware and Asus.Testing Borderlands 3 at the “Badass” preset, the Raider tied with Razer with 189 FPS for the highest 1080p number, clearly surpassing the Alienware (164 FPS) and Asus (156 FPS). This gap is wider than we saw in some of the other games.MSI’s Raider delivers excellent gaming performance, landing neck-and-neck with the larger Blade 18. Its RTX 5090 delivers marginal but measurable performance advantages over Alienware’s RTX 5080 system, which runs at the same maximum graphics power. That said, the differences might not be noticeable to most gamers; MSI also offers this laptop with RTX 5080 for about $600 less. The RTX 5090’s other differentiator is its larger 24GB frame buffer versus the RTX 5080’s 16GB, though the latter is already more than enough for today’s games.We stress test gaming laptops running 15 loops of the Metro Exodus stress test at RTX settings. During the test, the Raider averaged 142.3 FPS across all runs with almost no variance, starting the first loop at 143.5 FPS and finishing the final loop at 142.8 FPS. The Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus averaged 4.51 GHz on its P-cores and 2.59 GHz on its E-cores while the RTX 5090’s average boost clock was 1.99 GHz.Productivity Performance on the MSI Raider 16 Max HXWe evaluated the Raider with a Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus processor, 32GB of RAM, and a 1TB SSD. It’s notable MSI includes a PCIe 4.0 drive, not a PCIe 5.0 drive as we’d expect in a high-end gaming laptop. (One of its two M.2 slots supports PCIe 5.0 drives, MSI simply doesn’t equip it with a PCIe 5.0 drive.)Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)In Geekbench 6, these laptops are essentially neck-and-neck, though the refreshed Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus chips in the Raider and Alienware perform marginally better than the Core Ultra 9 275HX in the others. The real-world differences between these chips will likely go unnoticed.In our 25GB file transfer test, MSI’s Raider performed well below par, with 1,357.93 MBps against the next-fastest Asus, with 1,841.41 MBps, and nowhere near the Alienware’s 2,738.9 MBps.The Raider bounced back in Handbrake, completing the test in the shortest time at 1 minute and 51 seconds. The Alienware was the next quickest at an even two minutes, with the Razer (2:07) and Asus (2:24) trailing.Display on the MSI Raider 16 Max HXThe Raider’s 2560 x 1600 OLED display is one of its best features, offering a world-class picture for multimedia and everything else. Watching Blade Runner 2049, I noticed subtle creases on K’s trench coat, mesmerizing colors on Joi’s holograph, and the intricate detail in Wallace’s eyes. I also admired the searing brightness of my lightsaber and the feel of total blackness in caves and unlit rooms in Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order. The 240 Hz refresh rate translates to smooth scrolling and no frame tearing. For immersion in any kind of media, this screen is sure to please. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)These laptops use different screen technologies – OLED for the MSI and Alienware and mini-LED for the Asus and Razer. OLED demonstrates better color coverage, with the Raider’s 86.1% higher than Asus’ 81.2% and Razer’s 80% but not quite matching the Alienware’s 93.7%. However, the Raider’s 456-nit peak brightness was much sunnier than the Alienware’s 369 nits and practically tied the Asus and Razer.In our HDR testing, not shown in the charts, the Raider’s 40% average was 991 nits and 100% average was 600 nits.Keyboard and Touchpad on the MSI Raider 16 Max HXMSI’s keyboard prioritizes gaming and makes some curious layout choices in the process. There’s no left Fn key, which has been moved next to the arrow keys. Oddly, another slash key is there, too. The MSI Center app allows toggling the Windows key and/or swapping it with the Fn key, but those use to left-handed Fn and Windows key shortcuts will need to adjust. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The arrow keys are full-size but the three-column number pad isn’t, which has a strange layout and tiny keys – it probably should have been left out in favor of dedicated macro or media control keys. Macros can still be configured in the SteelSeries GG app, but nothing beats dedicated keys.Typing feel is quite satisfying, though, with a crisp keypress, just enough travel, and a rigid deck. I managed 119 words per minute with 99% accuracy in my usual MonkeyType run. A mechanical keyboard might further improve the typing experience; alas, MSI doesn’t offert one on this model, though Alienware does on its Aurora..Keyboard backlighting is also more than bright enough and customizable in per-key RGB fashion, with patterns and layered effects using SteelSeries GG. In another gaming flourish, the WASD and arrow keys are transparent and appear extra bright.MSI’s touchpad is also superb. Though it’s top-hinged rather than haptic, it clicks predictably and has plenty of surface area relative to the 16-inch display.Audio on the MSI Raider 16 Max HXMSI’s built-in speakers satisfy casual listening. User-facing in the screen hinge, they project well even if the laptop isn’t on a solid surface. Bass doesn’t thump but is present, adding a sense of immersion in games and movies. The included Nahimic audio app provides equalizers that make the speakers sound much fuller than they would otherwise be, though beyond enabling the basic preset for Movies, Music, or Gaming, I didn’t hear a benefit to tweaking the other settings, such as bass and treble boost.In Blade Runner 2049, I could hear the subtle strain in Lieutenant Joshi’s voice as she was being interrogated and the delicate patter of rainfall on the city streets. The Movie equalizer proved useful here, though I disabled the volume leveling feature as it made quiet scenes too loud. I also appreciated the audio in Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order, from the crunch of my footsteps over rocks to the piercing ignition of my lightsaber.Upgradeability of the MSI Raider 16 Max HXMSI simplified the Raider’s upgradeability by housing all common components under a dedicated upgrade panel, secured with two Philips-head screws. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Behind the panel are the two SODIMM slots and two M.2 2280 PCIe slots, making these upgrades simple. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Removing the entire bottom panel is necessary to access the 92 Whr battery and the M.2 2230 wireless card. After removing the Philips-head screws around the edges, all of which are the same length, I found there was one more in the center under the upgrade panel that was hard to spot. Finding a gap between the chassis halves where I could fit my trim removal tool proved challenging, but I was able to make one along the LED lightbar on the front edge. The panel popped free after I freed the clips along the edges. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Battery Life on the MSI Raider 16 Max HXOur battery test consists of web browsing, running OpenGL tests, and streaming videos with the screen at 150 nits while connected to Wi-Fi. The Raider distinguished itself with a commanding time of 8 hours and 34 minutes, over two hours more than the Asus (6:30) and totally outclassing the Alienware’s 3:33. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Heat on the MSI Raider 16 Max HXWe measure gaming laptop surface temperatures while running the 15 loops of our Metro Exodus stress test. During the test, the Raider peaked at 87 degrees Fahrenheit between the keyboard G and H keys, 74 F on the touchpad, and 109 F on the underside towards the exhaust vents. Internally, the Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus ran at 75 degrees Celsius across all cores while the RTX 5090 averaged 72 C.Fan noise is well-controlled on the Raider – while gaming, the laptop’s built-in speakers are able to overpower the fan noise, and the fans are hardly noticeable for everyday tasks.Webcam on the MSI Raider 16 Max HXMSI’s webcam produces satisfactory video quality. Though its 1080p resolution is the minimum accepted these days, it accurately exposed my face despite the window behind me and didn’t show any grain, even in the shadows of my black t-shirt. The webcam also includes an infrared (IR) sensor for facial logins with Windows Hello and a sliding privacy shutter.Software and Warranty on the MSI Raider 16 Max HXMinimal software is included on the Raider. MSI Center provides hardware monitoring, support access, and settings: the Windows key can be toggled and/or swapped with the Fn key, and there’s a toggle for USB power share. The app also provides power profiles for Extreme Performance, Balanced, or Eco (Silent), and features a MUX switch for disabling the onboard graphics.Tinkering with the keyboard and external lighting is done through the familiar SteelSeries GG app, which sees the Raider as a device. Preset lighting patterns and color themes are included, and the app provides in-depth settings for creating your own, including layered effects.MSI backs the Raider with a one-year warranty.MSI Raider 16 Max HX ConfigurationsMSI offers three Raider 16 Max HX configurations through Micro Center, all of which feature a 240 Hz OLED display, Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus, 32GB of RAM, and a 1TB SSD. The only difference between them is the GPU, with the base RTX 5070 Ti model (B2WJ-002US) commanding $2,999, the RTX 5080 $3,499 (B2WI-003US), and our tested RTX 5090 model $4,099 (B2WH-004US). Our model was discounted to $3,499 at review time.MSI’s pricing is competitive among RTX 5090 laptops. The ROG Strix Scar 16 was $4,499 directly from Asus, though it included a 2TB SSD. Meanwhile, Alienware’s 16 Area-51 listed at $4,699, though a flash sale temporarily brought it down to $3,699.Bottom LineMSI’s Raider 16 Max HX delivers an elite gaming experience in a laptop: its Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus and RTX 5090 push high frame rates with ease, the OLED panel looks superb, and the system stays impressively cool and quiet under load. Customizable lighting and practical connectivity round out its appeal.At this price, though, a PCIe 5.0 SSD and more premium chassis materials would feel more appropriate. Compared with Alienware’s 16-inch Area-51, the Raider lacks a mechanical keyboard and isn’t quite as showy, but it counters with far better battery life and a noticeably brighter display. All in all, the Raider is a compelling option for anyone with the money to spend on it.MSI Raider 16 Max HX: Price Comparison $3,499.99View No price informationCheck Best Buy No price informationCheck AmazonWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricespowered by
history.author: Charles Jefferies
history.titleZh: 微星 Raider 16 Max HX 评测：精英级游戏性能
history.descriptionZh:
MSI推出的Raider 16 Max HX游戏笔记本电脑（起售价2999美元）是目前速度最快的游戏本之一，搭载英特尔Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus处理器，最高可选配Nvidia RTX 5090显卡。除了强大的性能，这款笔记本还配备了色彩鲜艳的OLED显示屏，实际电池续航远超预期。虽然它缺少机械键盘且存储速度可以更快，但如果你预算充足，它依然是一个值得考虑的选择——我们测试的RTX 5090顶配版售价高达4099美元。

**设计与规格**

Raider 16 Max HX的外观毫不掩饰其游戏血统：厚实的机身、炫酷的LED灯带以及机盖上的图案都表明它的身份。机身前缘的灯条和机盖背面的盾形徽标可通过SteelSeries GG应用程序自定义颜色和效果。整体设计在激进与克制之间取得了平衡，关闭灯光时并不会过于张扬。尺寸为14.29 x 10.62 x 1.14英寸，与Alienware 16 Area-51和Asus ROG Strix Scar 16相当，但5.73磅的重量是三者中最轻的。为了减重，机身主要采用塑料材质，虽然整体结构感觉坚固，但顶盖是铝制的，感觉较薄且柔韧性超出预期，不过铰链顺滑稳固。接口布局实用，左侧提供两个Thunderbolt 4和SD卡槽，右侧有两个USB-A 3.2 Gen 2和音频插孔，背面有2.5Gbps以太网、HDMI 2.1和另一个USB-A口。400W电源适配器通过背部的专用接口连接。内部采用Killer BE1750网卡，支持Wi-Fi 7和蓝牙5.4。

核心规格方面，测试机型搭载Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus处理器、Nvidia RTX 5090显卡（24GB GDDR7显存，最高175W功耗）、32GB DDR5-6400内存和1TB PCIe 4.0固态硬盘（Micron 2500）。显示屏为16英寸2560x1600 OLED，240Hz刷新率。

**游戏与图形性能**

在原生2560x1600分辨率下运行《F1 24》（Ultra High设置，关闭DLSS），帧率稳定在90-95fps。与对比机型——Alienware 16 Area-51（RTX 5080 175W，$4,309）、Asus ROG Strix Scar 16（RTX 5080 175W，$3,299）和上代Razor Blade 18（RTX 5090 175W，$5,199）相比，所有机型CPU和GPU功耗级别相同（175W）。在1080p和原生分辨率的多款游戏测试中，Raider表现出色。

在《Shadow of the Tomb Raider》最高画质下，1080p帧率197fps，仅次于Razer的213fps，高于Alienware的191fps；原生分辨率下134fps，明显领先于Alienware的116fps和Asus的112fps，证明RTX 5090相比RTX 5080有轻微但可测量的性能优势。在《Cyberpunk 2077》光线追踪Ultra预设下，RTX 5090机型继续领先，Raider以70fps（1080p）和42fps（原生）紧随Razer之后，但大幅领先Alienware和Asus。《Red Dead Redemption 2》中等画质下，Raider以130fps（1080p）和90fps（原生）表现强劲。《Borderlands 3》Badass预设下，Raider与Razer并列第一（189fps），显著超越Alienware（164fps）和Asus（156fps）。

总体而言，Raider的游戏性能出色，与体积更大的Blade 18不相上下。RTX 5090相比RTX 5080的优势虽然有限，但可测量，且其更大的24GB显存容量（vs 16GB）是潜在优势，尽管16GB已足够当前使用。在Metro Exodus压力测试的15次循环中，平均帧率142.3fps，几乎没有波动，CPU P核平均频率4.51GHz，GPU平均加速频率1.99GHz。

**生产力性能**

虽然Geekbench 6跑分与对手几乎持平，但Raider在Handbrake视频转码测试中以1分51秒的成绩领先所有对手。然而，在25GB文件传输测试中，其PCIe 4.0 SSD的写入速度仅为1357.93 MB/s，远低于Asus和Alienware，这是其最大短板之一——MSI没有标配PCIe 5.0 SSD，虽然主板上有一个M.2插槽支持PCIe 5.0。

**显示屏**

2560x1600 OLED面板是Raider的最佳特性之一。播放《银翼杀手2049》时细节丰富，色彩迷人；《星球大战绝地：陨落的武士团》中光剑亮度耀眼，黑暗场景呈现纯正黑色。240Hz刷新率带来流畅滚动和无撕裂体验。色彩覆盖率达到86.1% DCI-P3，略逊于Alienware的93.7%，但峰值亮度高达456尼特，远高于Alienware的369尼特，与Asus和Razer的mini-LED屏相当。在HDR测试中，40%窗口平均亮度达991尼特，100%窗口达600尼特。

**键盘与触摸板**

键盘专为游戏优化，但布局有些奇特：左侧没有Fn键，被移到方向键旁边，还有一个多余的斜杠键。方向键是全尺寸的，但数字小键盘布局奇怪且按键太小。按键手感清脆，键程适中，底板坚固，打字体验令人满意，我在MonkeyType测试中取得119wpm、99%准确率。遗憾的是，这款机型不提供机械键盘选项。键盘支持每键RGB自定义背光，WASD和方向键采用透明设计，亮度更高。触摸板虽非触觉反馈式，但上铰链设计点击反馈良好，面积充足。

**音频与散热**

内置扬声器位于屏幕铰链处，声音投射良好，即使在非平面上也能清晰传播。低音虽然不够震撼，但存在感足够，增强了沉浸感。Nahimic音频应用提供均衡器，开启预设后声音更加饱满。在《银翼杀手2049》中能清晰听到人声中的细微紧张和雨滴声。散热方面，在Metro Exodus压力测试中，键盘最高温87°F（G和H键之间），触摸板74°F，底部排气口109°F。CPU平均75°C，GPU平均72°C。风扇噪音控制良好，游戏时扬声器能盖过风扇声，日常使用几乎听不到。

**升级性与电池续航**

升级非常方便：一个专用盖板（两个螺丝）下可访问两个SO-DIMM内存插槽和两个M.2 2280 PCIe插槽。更换电池和无线网卡需要拆下整个底盖，过程稍复杂。电池续航表现出色：在150尼特亮度、Wi-Fi连接下进行网页浏览、OpenGL测试和视频流播放，续航时间达8小时34分钟，远超Asus的6小时30分钟和Alienware的3小时33分钟。

**摄像头、软件与配置**

1080p红外摄像头支持Windows Hello面部识别，带有物理隐私快门，画质可满足日常视频通话。预装软件精简：MSI Center提供硬件监控、电源模式切换和MUX开关；SteelSeries GG用于自定义键盘和外置灯光。MSI提供一年质保。目前通过Micro Center有三种配置：RTX 5070 Ti版（$2,999）、RTX 5080版（$3,499）、RTX 5090版（$4,099），均配备32GB内存和1TB SSD。在RTX 5090机型中，其定价具有竞争力。

**总结**

MSI Raider 16 Max HX提供了顶级的游戏体验：Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus和RTX 5090轻松驱动高帧率，OLED显示效果惊艳，系统在负载下保持冷静安静。可定制灯光和实用接口进一步提升了吸引力。然而，在这个价位，用户理应得到PCIe 5.0 SSD和更高级的机身材料。与Alienware 16 Area-51相比，它没有机械键盘，外观不那么张扬，但以更长的电池续航和更亮的显示屏作为补偿。总而言之，对于预算充足的高端玩家，Raider是一个极具竞争力的选择。

history.title: Intel's Project Firefly creates sub-$600 laptops to compete with Apple's MacBook Neo — leverages China's smartphone manufacturing blueprint to produce affordable Wildcat Lake systems
history.description: Intel China has unveiled Project Firefly, which borrows a page from China's smartphone supply chain to produce budget-friendly laptops that leverage Wildcat Lake processors.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 10 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Apple’s MacBook Neo has redefined how we view budget laptops and has easily earned a spot on our list of best laptops. But Windows fans may finally see some competition with their go-to OS. Intel held a Core Series 3 processor (codenamed Wildcat Lake) launch event in China today, and alongside the launch came the announcement of Project Firefly (via ITHome). Project Firefly is an initiative to challenge the MacBook Neo by leveraging China’s efficient smartphone supply chain, allowing manufacturers to mass-produce Wildcat Lake-powered devices at the lowest possible cost.Go deeper with TH Premium: CPU (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU scaling with DLSSRyzen to the top: How AMD innovated in the gaming CPU marketHow ARM is working its way into PCsAMD CES 2026 gaming trends press Q&A roundtable transcriptWhen it comes to laptops, every vendor does its own thing with design and componentry. Project Firefly aims to revolutionize the process by streamlining laptop design and manufacturing, taking inspiration from the smartphone industry. In the world of smartphones, companies utilize standardized components and modular designs. Project Firefly wants to apply this model to laptops by introducing a universal standard and potentially smaller interfaces that manufacturers can use across different brands and models.Factories can churn out millions of units with remarkable speed and cost-effectiveness. The new model should significantly drive down research, development, and production costs.Latest Videos From You may like First Intel Wildcat Lake-equipped laptop spotted — features 11W fanless mode, 35W PL2 Apple announces $599 MacBook Neo running A18 Pro chip Intel launches Wildcat Lake as Core Series 3 for value laptops and edge systems Project Firefly specifically targets the budget segment of the laptop market. Its impact will be noticeable in laptops equipped with Intel’s new Core Series 3 chips, not to be confused with the more premium Core Ultra Series 3 (codenamed Panther Lake) lineup. Announced last month, Wildcat Lake, which is based on Intel’s 18A process node, features between five and six Cougar Cove P-cores, notably without Hyper-Threading support.Gao Song, Intel’s Vice President and General Manager of Software Engineering and Client Products, took the stage at the Wildcat Lake launch event in China to showcase a Project Firefly reference design laptop. The device immediately turned heads with its vibrant orange exterior, displaying the Intel Color branding on the top cover. The reference model flaunts a lightweight and thin profile (0.43 inches or 11mm) with a minimalist “Clean-D” design, as Intel calls it.Intel’s partners, including major players like Asus, HP, and Honor, have already begun rolling out their Wildcat Lake-powered laptops in the Chinese market, with pre-tax prices ranging from $571 to $662. Chinese manufacturer Chuwi has also introduced the UniBook, which comes in at an especially aggressive MSRP of $449. However, it’s important to note that while these laptops benefit from the new Wildcat Lake platform, they are not products of Project Firefly. The very first laptop to arrive under Project Firefly will be Lenovo’s upcoming Lecoo Air 14.Besides taking on Apple’s MacBook Neo, Project Firefly will also challenge Arm-powered laptops and Chromebooks, which are also aimed at budget-conscious consumers. If Intel succeeds in delivering high-quality, affordable laptops through Project Firefly, it. would offer buyers the option to stay within their x86 comfort zone.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Zhiye Liu
history.titleZh: 英特尔Project Firefly推出低于600美元的笔记本以抗衡苹果MacBook Neo——借鉴中国智能手机制造蓝图，打造平价Wildcat Lake系统
history.descriptionZh:
苹果推出的MacBook Neo重新定义了低价笔记本市场，并成功跻身最受欢迎笔记本之列。如今，Windows阵营终于有望迎来旗鼓相当的对手。英特尔今日在中国举办了Core Series 3处理器（代号Wildcat Lake）的发布活动，同时宣布了名为“Project Firefly”的计划。该计划旨在借鉴中国高效的智能手机供应链，让制造商能以最低成本大规模生产搭载Wildcat Lake芯片的设备，从而正面挑战MacBook Neo。

Project Firefly的核心创新在于彻底变革笔记本的设计与制造流程——它从智能手机行业获得灵感，引入标准化组件和模块化设计。具体而言，该计划希望建立一套通用标准，并可能采用更小的接口方案，使得不同品牌和型号的笔记本都能共享相同的基础架构。工厂因此能够以惊人的速度和成本效益批量生产数百万台设备，大幅降低研发、设计和生产成本。

该计划瞄准的是笔记本市场的低价位区间，主要作用于搭载英特尔全新Core Series 3芯片（注意：不要与更高级的Core Ultra Series 3系列混淆）的产品。Wildcat Lake基于英特尔18A制程工艺，集成了5到6个Cougar Cove性能核心（P-core），但值得注意的是，这些核心不支持超线程技术。该处理器支持11W无风扇模式和35W PL2功耗设定。

在Wildcat Lake的中国发布会上，英特尔副总裁兼软件工程与客户端产品总经理高松登台展示了一款Project Firefly参考设计笔记本。该设备凭借亮橙色外壳和顶盖上的英特尔彩色品牌标识立刻吸引了眼球。参考机型采用轻薄设计，厚度仅为11毫米（0.43英寸），并采用了英特尔所谓的“Clean-D”极简设计风格。

目前，包括华硕、惠普和荣耀在内的多家主流合作伙伴已开始在中国市场推出基于Wildcat Lake的笔记本，税前价格在571美元至662美元之间。中国厂商驰为也推出了UniBook，建议零售价仅为449美元，极具竞争力。不过，这些产品虽然受益于Wildcat Lake平台，但并非Project Firefly计划下的产物。真正第一款属于Project Firefly的笔记本将是联想即将推出的Lecoo Air 14。

除了对标苹果MacBook Neo，Project Firefly也将冲击Arm架构笔记本和Chromebook——它们同样瞄准预算有限的消费者。如果英特尔能通过该计划成功推出高品质且价格亲民的笔记本，将为用户提供继续留在x86“舒适区”的选择。

history.title: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang at Dell Technologies World: ‘Demand Is Going Parabolic, Utterly Parabolic’
history.description: Agentic AI inference at one-tenth the cost per token with NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72. Agent sandboxes run 50% faster on NVIDIA Vera than traditional CPUs — while enterprise data queries are up to 3x faster with the Vera CPU. And 5,000 enterprises like Lilly, Samsung and Honeywell are running AI workloads on Dell AI Factories […]
history.content: Agentic AI inference at one-tenth the cost per token with NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72. Agent sandboxes run 50% faster on NVIDIA Vera than traditional CPUs — while enterprise data queries are up to 3x faster with the Vera CPU. And 5,000 enterprises like Lilly, Samsung and Honeywell are running AI workloads on Dell AI Factories with NVIDIA, turning ambition into production at scale. That’s the picture Michael Dell painted Monday morning at Dell Technologies World. Dell sized the stakes: Worldwide AI infrastructure spending could reach $3-4 trillion by 2030, with token consumption projected to grow 3,400% in the same window. “There is a massive AI investment boom thats already underway, and a productivity boom is beginning, and in some companies, including ours,” Dell said. “The rate of change has gone parabolic, and it’s not slowing down.” Then, the Dell chairman and CEO welcomed NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang to the keynote stage — with a look at the NVIDIA portfolio behind him, from a deskside Dell Pro Max with GB10 workstation to a Dell PowerRack with NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72. “We’ve now arrived at the era of useful AI, which is the reason why demand is going parabolic, utterly parabolic,” Huang said. “What took months now takes weeks. What took weeks now takes days. And what takes days now takes hours. It’s a big deal in productivity, but a gigantic leap in computation requirements.” The message: Enterprise AI has moved past pilots into agentic AI and inference deployments at scale. The platform for what’s next is the Dell AI Factory with NVIDIA — running frontier models and autonomous agents securely behind the enterprise perimeter. A New AI Factory for the Agentic Era The accelerated computing news leads the refresh: The Dell PowerEdge XE9812, built on NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72, delivers up to 10x lower cost-per-token than NVIDIA Blackwell for massive-scale agentic AI inferencing. It’s joined by PowerEdge XE9880L, XE9885L and XE9882L servers — the first Dell systems built on NVIDIA HGX Rubin NVL8, supporting up to 144 GPUs per rack with 100% direct liquid-cooled compute nodes and up to 10x the performance of HGX B200. In addition, networking gets the new Dell PowerSwitch portfolio with NVIDIA Quantum-X800 InfiniBand, featuring liquid-cooled, co-packaged optics and NVIDIA Spectrum-6 Ethernet. Dell is also introducing Dell PowerRack, a fully integrated system – compute, networking and storage engineered as one – with thermal design, power management and software optimization built to work together from the ground up. The result is accelerated AI and high-performance computing workloads at enterprise scale, without the integration overhead of component assembly. On the CPU side, Dell PowerEdge M9822 and R9822 servers bring NVIDIA Vera CPUs to the enterprise AI factory. Purpose-built for agentic AI, Vera runs data pipelines, analytics, sandboxed tools and code workloads where each step waits on the last. With 1.2 TB/s memory bandwidth and predictable performance under load, Vera completes agentic workloads 50% faster than x86 processors, helping PowerEdge systems increase AI factory output with faster agent responses and shorter feedback loops. “Vera CPU has the highest single-threaded performance of any CPU in the world,” Huang said. “It has three times the memory bandwidth — as a result, Starburst, DuckDB, all these databases run incredibly fast, because the agents are pounding on the databases, so the CPU had better be super fast.” Starburst, a new data engine in the Dell AI Data Platform with NVIDIA, delivers 3x faster query throughput on NVIDIA Vera CPU for large-scale SQL analytics. Enterprise data provides the fuel for the AI factory. Dell’s update to its AI Data Platform with NVIDIA centers on accelerated data engines built on NVIDIA CUDA-X libraries — including cuDF for structured data and cuVS for unstructured data. Multiple customers for Dell AI Factory with NVIDIA were featured in the keynote. Diogo Rau, executive vice president and chief information and digital officer of Lilly, joined early in the keynote — discussing Lilly’s AI-driven advancements and innovation in life sciences, powered by AI infrastructure deployed at scale with Dell and NVIDIA. He described technology as key to delivering cutting-edge science, at scale. “I think we’re on the verge of maybe being able to end disease as we know it,” Rau said. “Something like that was completely unimaginable 20 years ago, but today we can imagine it.” A video from Samsung followed — highlighting use cases for R&D chip design and manufacturing running on Dell AI Factory with NVIDIA. Honeywell chief technology officer Suresh Venkatarayalu joined Michael Dell to walk through the company’s move from public cloud to on-premises AI — using the Dell AI Factory and Dell AI Data Platform with NVIDIA for industrial AI use cases, digital twins and automation from data center to the edge. “For me, partnering with Dell and NVIDIA is not just about getting infrastructure,” Venkatarayalu said. It’s the full AI stack, he explained: scalable, secured and trusted by customers. And in financial services, Hudson River Trading, the algorithmic trading firm, is expanding its Dell deployment to power AI-driven research — running Dell PowerEdge XE9685L servers with NVIDIA accelerated computing and NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet to scale with the firm’s data, models and ambition. Agents and Models on Premises — Securely Dell’s own AI adoption survey, cited from the keynote stage, found that 67% of AI workloads now run outside the cloud — on premises, on device, at the edge or in colocation — and that 88% of respondents are running at least one AI workload on premises. The on-premises AI announcements answered a question Dell put to the room directly: “How do you deploy the world’s best AI models where you need them, with security and governance built in?” The answer rests on NVIDIA Confidential Computing — delivered together with Fortanix, Google, Red Hat and other partners — as the foundation for securely deploying frontier models inside the enterprise without exposing model IP or data. This enables enterprises to protect AI models and sensitive data in use while capturing the token efficiency, performance and cost advantages of on-premises AI infrastructure. Frontier Proprietary Models, Protected by Confidential Computing Google Distributed Cloud (GDC) with Gemini 3.0 is now available in preview on Dell PowerEdge XE9780 servers, accelerated by NVIDIA Blackwell and secured by NVIDIA Confidential Computing — giving enterprises a private confidential computing environment for advanced AI. SpaceXAI will also bring the latest SpaceXAI models on premises to the Dell AI Factory, with NVIDIA Confidential Computing keeping model weights and enterprise data protected end to end. Open Frontier Models, Running Natively on the Dell AI Factory NVIDIA Nemotron models — frontier-class open intelligence — run on Dell AI Factory infrastructure for enterprises that want open weight models tuned to their own domains and data. Reflection’s open source frontier AI models are also coming on premises, purpose-built for regulated industries, governments and sovereign entities. Additional open models — MiniMax-M2.7, DeepSeek Pro, DeepSeek-V4, GLM 5.1 and Kimi K2.6 with NVIDIA NVFP4 optimization — are available on the Dell Enterprise Hub on Hugging Face, joining Gemma 4, NVIDIA Nemotron Super 3, Mistral Small 4 and Arcee Trinity-Large-Thinking. In this new agentic era, enterprises also need agents to work securely across the hybrid and on-premises environments where their data, systems and workflows already live. OpenAI Codex will connect with the Dell AI Data Platform, to help customers bring Codex closer to the internal context that makes agents useful: codebases, documentation, business systems, operational knowledge and team workflows. Dell and OpenAI will also explore how Codex can connect with the Dell AI Factory. Ecosystem of Software Partners Dell announced several new software partnerships for common enterprise AI use cases ranging from agentic AI and code assistants to computer vision. This includes Palantir’s sovereign AI OS reference architecture with NVIDIA, announced in March, which now runs on Dell infrastructure — for on-premises deployment of Palantir Ontology and AIP, integrated with the NVIDIA Sovereign AI OS Reference Architecture. In addition, ServiceNow customers will be able to leverage the Dell AI Factory to bring together infrastructure and enterprise workflow automation, enabling organizations to discover, govern and operationalize AI focused on business outcomes. Dell also announced new solutions with a wide range of AI leaders and software innovators —including Fogsphere, Ipsotek, Mistral AI, Poolside and Uneeq — as well as security partnerships with CrowdStrike and Fortanix. Agents From Deskside to Data Center The most personal news: Dell Deskside Agentic AI with the NVIDIA NemoClaw stack, NVIDIA OpenShell runtime and NVIDIA Nemotron open models run on Dell Pro Max with GB10 and GB300 powered by the NVIDIA Grace Blackwell architecture, as well as Dell Pro Precision systems powered by NVIDIA RTX PRO Blackwell workstation platforms. The customization layer: NVIDIA Nemotron, NVIDIA Agent Toolkit and NVIDIA NeMoClaw — the agent orchestration harness Huang described on stage as the connective layer between local models and enterprise data — provide the foundation for building enterprise autonomous agents, enabling organizations to customize models, orchestrate agent workflows and securely connect agents to enterprise data and tools. The security layer: NVIDIA OpenShell — an open source runtime for development and deployment of autonomous agents with security and privacy controls — enables corporate policy enforcement at the infrastructure layer and is integrated with leading enterprise software platforms. NVIDIA OpenShell is now supported across the entire Dell AI Factory with NVIDIA, giving developers a secure runtime to build, deploy and govern AI agents from workstations to servers. Dell also highlighted support for the NVIDIA AI-Q Blueprint, giving enterprises a reference example to deploy multi-agent workflows for deep research — accelerating the path from development to pilot to production. Day Two On day two of Dell Technologies World, Dell’s Chief Operations Officer Jeff Clarke and Infrastructure Solutions Group President Arthur Lewis will go deeper on NVIDIA Vera CPU, Vera Rubin, Confidential Computing and Nemotron — with a live demo of Dell Deskside Agentic AI. The themes Huang and Dell set Monday — safe, long-running agents, full-stack factories, secure on-premises deployment — set up the broader announcements NVIDIA will bring to GTC Taipei at COMPUTEX, running June 1-4.
history.author: NVIDIA Writers
history.titleZh: 英伟达CEO黄仁勋在戴尔科技世界大会：需求呈抛物线式增长，完全呈抛物线式增长
history.descriptionZh:
在Dell Technologies World大会上，戴尔科技创始人兼CEO迈克尔·戴尔与英伟达创始人兼CEO黄仁勋共同描绘了企业AI进入“有用AI”时代的新蓝图。全球AI基础设施支出预计到2030年将达3-4万亿美元，而token消耗量同期将增长3400%。两人强调，AI需求已呈“抛物线式”增长，企业AI正从试点阶段全面转向智能体AI（agentic AI）和大规模推理部署。戴尔AI工厂（Dell AI Factory with NVIDIA）成为下一代企业AI的核心平台，能在企业安全边界内运行前沿模型和自主智能体，将“需要数月的工作缩短为几周，几周变为几天，几天变为几小时”。

**核心技术原理与创新点**

- **新一代加速计算平台**：戴尔推出基于NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72的PowerEdge XE9812，专为大规模智能体AI推理设计。相比NVIDIA Blackwell，其每token成本降低高达10倍，实现“十分之一的成本获得相同推理能力”。同时发布的PowerEdge XE9880L/9885L/9882L是首批基于NVIDIA HGX Rubin NVL8的系统，每机架支持最多144颗GPU，采用100%直接液冷计算节点，性能可达HGX B200的10倍。
- **Vera CPU的突破**：戴尔推出搭载NVIDIA Vera CPU的PowerEdge M9822和R9822服务器。Vera CPU拥有全球最高的单线程性能，1.2 TB/s内存带宽，在负载下性能可预测。它针对智能体AI工作负载优化，能比传统x86处理器快50%完成智能体任务（包括数据流水线、分析、沙箱工具和代码执行），从而缩短智能体响应时间和反馈循环。黄仁勋表示：“Vera CPU拥有全球最高的单线程性能，三倍内存带宽，使得Starburst、DuckDB等数据库运行极快。”
- **新型网络与集成系统**：戴尔推出搭载NVIDIA Quantum-X800 InfiniBand（含液冷共封装光学器件）和Spectrum-6以太网的新PowerSwitch交换机组合，以及完全集成计算、网络和存储的Dell PowerRack系统，从设计之初就统一考虑热设计、电源管理和软件优化，消除组件集成的额外开销。

**性能参数与对比数据**

- 成本效率：Vera Rubin NVL72对比Blackwell，每token推理成本降低10倍。
- CPU性能：Vera CPU完成智能体工作负载比x86处理器快50%；Starburst数据引擎在Vera CPU上SQL分析查询吞吐量提升3倍。
- 内存带宽：Vera CPU达1.2 TB/s，为业界最高。
- 密度与冷却：HGX Rubin NVL8系统每机架支持144颗GPU，100%直接液冷。
- 性能对比：HGX Rubin系统性能可达HGX B200的10倍。

**技术影响与应用场景**

- **企业推理与智能体AI**：戴尔AI工厂支持大规模智能体推理，已服务5000家企业（如礼来、三星、霍尼韦尔）。礼来CIO表示，借助戴尔和英伟达的AI基础设施，生命科学正接近“终结疾病”的愿景。三星用于芯片设计和制造研发，霍尼韦尔从公有云迁移至本地AI，用于工业数字孪生和自动化。金融领域，算法交易公司Hudson River Trading扩展戴尔部署，运行AI驱动的研究。
- **安全本地部署**：戴尔调查显示67%的AI工作负载运行在云端之外（本地、边缘或托管），88%受访者至少有1个AI工作负载在本地运行。为此，戴尔与Fortanix、Google、Red Hat等合作，基于NVIDIA机密计算技术，实现在企业内安全部署前沿模型，保护模型IP和数据。Google Distributed Cloud with Gemini 3.0已在Dell PowerEdge XE9780上预览，由Blackwell加速并受机密计算保护。SpaceXAI也将通过机密计算在本地部署其最新模型。
- **开放模型与智能体生态**：NVIDIA Nemotron开放智能模型、Reflection开源前沿AI模型、MiniMax、DeepSeek、GLM、Kimi等开放模型均可通过Hugging Face上的Dell Enterprise Hub获取。OpenAI Codex将连接戴尔AI数据平台，使智能体靠近企业内部代码库、文档和业务系统。此外，Palantir、ServiceNow、CrowdStrike、Mistral AI等软件合作伙伴推出针对智能体AI、代码助手、计算机视觉的解决方案。
- **从桌面到数据中心**：戴尔推出Deskside Agentic AI，在Dell Pro Max（搭载GB10/GB300，基于Grace Blackwell架构）和Pro Precision工作站（基于RTX PRO Blackwell）上运行NVIDIA NeMoClaw、OpenShell和Nemotron模型。NVIDIA OpenShell作为开源安全运行时，在整个戴尔AI工厂中支持智能体开发、部署和治理。NVIDIA AI-Q Blueprint为企业提供多智能体深度研究工作流的参考示例。

**总结**：Dell Technologies World第二天，戴尔COO Jeff Clarke和ISG总裁Arthur Lewis将进一步深入演示NVIDIA Vera CPU、Vera Rubin、机密计算和Nemotron。这些宣布为英伟达在6月1-4日台北GTC/COMPUTEX上的更广泛发布奠定了基础。戴尔与英伟达构建的AI工厂，正通过降低推理成本、提升智能体性能、强化安全合规，推动企业AI从实验走向大规模生产。

history.title: Vera Arrives: NVIDIA’s First CPU Built for Agents Lands at Top AI Labs
history.description: The first NVIDIA Vera CPUs arrived at three of the world's leading AI labs on Friday — Anthropic in San Francisco, OpenAI in Mission Bay, SpaceXAI in Palo Alto — followed by a delivery to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure in Santa Clara on Monday. NVIDIA Vice President of Hyperscale and High-Performance Computing Ian Buck hand-delivered them.

history.titleZh: Vera 到来：英伟达首款为智能体打造的 CPU 登陆顶尖 AI 实验室
history.descriptionZh: 首批NVIDIA Vera CPU于周五送达全球三家领先AI实验室——位于旧金山的Anthropic、米慎湾的OpenAI以及帕洛阿尔托的SpaceXAI，并于周一交付至圣克拉拉的Oracle云基础设施。NVIDIA超大规模与高性能计算副总裁伊恩·巴克亲自送货上门。
